title: Date
subtitle: An absolute point in time, represented as seconds since the epoch.

members.description:
Return a new Date with the receiver's value plus an amount
    of time specified by aDuration object to the receiver.
Add aDuration to the receiver. Returns self.

Return a new Date with the receiver's value minus an amount of time
    specified by aDuration to the receiver. Returns self.
Subtract aDuration from the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: asAtomDate
members.description: Returns the date formatted as a valid atom date (rfc4287) in the system's timezone.

members.name: asNumber
members.description: Returns the date as seconds since 1970 UTC.

members.name: asSerialization
members.description:
Returns a serialization (sequence) of the date that allows for perfect
    reconstruction of the timestamp.

members.name: asString
members.description:
Returns a string representation of the receiver using the receivers format. If the optionalFormatString argument is present, the receiver's format is set to it first. Formatting is according to ANSI C date formatting rules. 

%a abbreviated weekday name (Sun, Mon, etc.)
%A full weekday name (Sunday, Monday, etc.)
%b abbreviated month name (Jan, Feb, etc.)
%B full month name (January, February, etc.)
%c full date and time string
%d day of the month as two-digit decimal integer (01-31)
%H hour as two-digit 24-hour clock decimal integer (00-23)
%I hour as two-digit 12-hour clock decimal integer (01-12)
%m month as a two-digit decimal integer (01-12)
%M minute as a two-digit decimal integer (00-59)
%p either "AM" or "PM"
%S second as a two-digit decimal integer (00-59)
%U number of week in the year as two-digit decimal integer (00-52)
with Sunday considered as first day of the week
%w weekday as one-digit decimal integer (0-6) with Sunday as 0
%W number of week in the year as two-digit decimal integer (00-52)
with Monday considered as first day of the week
%x full date string (no time); in the C locale, this is equivalent
to "%m/%d/%y".
%y year without century as two-digit decimal number (00-99)
%Y year with century as four-digit decimal number
%Z time zone name (e.g. EST);
null string if no time zone can be obtained
%% stands for '%' character in output string.

members.name: asUTC
members.description: Changes the timezone of this date to utc

members.name: clock
members.description:
Returns a number containing the number of seconds
    of processor time since the beginning of the program or -1 if unavailable.

members.name: convertToLocal
members.description: Converts self date from a UTC date to the equivalent local date

members.name: convertToUTC
members.description: Converts self from a local date to the equivalent UTC date

members.name: convertToZone
members.description:
Converts self to an equivalent data in a zone with offset (minutes west) and
    DST (true, false).

members.name: copy
members.description: Sets the receiver to be the same date as aDate. Returns self.

members.name: cpuSecondsToRun
members.description:
Evaluates message and returns a Number whose value
    is the cpu seconds taken to do the evaluation.

members.name: day
members.description: Returns a number containing the day of the month of the receiver.

members.name: format
members.description:
Returns the format string for the receiver. The default is "%Y-%m-%d
    %H:%M:%S %Z".

members.name: fromNumber
members.description: Sets the receiver to be aNumber seconds since 1970.

members.name: fromSerialization
members.description: Sets the date based on the serialization sequence.  Return self.

members.name: fromString
members.description:
Sets the receiver to the date specified by aString as parsed according to
    the given formatString. See the Date asString method for formatting rules.
    Returns self.

members.name: gmtOffset
members.description: Returns the system's timezone string. E.g., +1300 or -0500.

members.name: gmtOffsetSeconds
members.description: Returns the system's seconds east of UTC.

members.name: hour
members.description: Returns a number containing the hour of the day(0-23) of the receiver.

members.name: isDST
members.description: Returns true if the Date is set to use DST.  Posix only.

members.name: isDaylightSavingsTime
members.description:
Returns self if Daylight Saving Time is in effect for the receiver,
    otherwise returns Nil.

members.name: isPast
members.description: Returns true if the receiver is a date in the past.

members.name: isToday
members.description: Returns true if the receiver's date is today's date.

members.name: isValidTime
members.description:
Returns self if the specified time is valid, otherwise returns Nil.
    A negative value will count back; i.e., a value of -5 for the hour,
    will count back 5 hours to return a value of 19. No adjustment is
    done for values above 24.

members.name: minute
members.description: Returns a number containing the minute of the hour(0-59) of the receiver.

members.name: month
members.description: Returns a number containing the month(1-12) of the year of the receiver.

members.name: now
members.description: Sets the receiver to the current time. Returns self.

members.name: print
members.description: Prints the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: second
members.description:
Returns a number containing the seconds of the minute(0-59) of the receiver.
    This number may contain fractions of seconds.

members.name: secondsSince
members.description: Returns a number of seconds of between aDate and the receiver.

members.name: secondsSinceNow
members.description: Returns the number of seconds since aDate.

members.name: secondsToRun
members.description: Evaluates message and returns a Number whose value is the number of seconds taken to do the evaluation

members.name: setDay
members.description: Sets the day of the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: setGmtOffset
members.description: Set the number of minutes west of GMT for this Date's zone

members.name: setHour
members.description: Sets the hour of the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: setMinute
members.description: Sets the minute of the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: setMonth
members.description: Sets the month(1-12) of the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: setSecond
members.description: Sets the second of the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: setYear
members.description: Sets the year of the receiver.

members.name: today
members.description:
Set the receiver to the current date, no time information
	is included. See `now' for the current date and time.

members.name: year
members.description: Returns a number containing the year of the receiver.

members.name: zone
members.description: Returns a string containing the system's time zone code.
